label: MQTT to EnergyID Webhook Bridge
info:
MQTT to EnergyID incoming webhook bridge.

Subscribes to configured MQTT topic families, auto-discovers devices from MQTT topics, registers each device against EnergyID via /hello with a deterministic claimCode, and batches readings to the per-device webhookUrl.

State lives in two global keys:
  global.eid.registry   - { deviceId: { ...meta, claimed, headers, webhookUrl } }
  global.eid.buffers    - { deviceId: [ {ts, el}, ... ] }

Required environment variables:
  MQTT_BROKER_HOST=<mqtt-broker-host>
  MQTT_BROKER_PORT=8883
  MQTT_BROKER_USER=<mqtt-user>
  MQTT_BROKER_PASSWORD=<mqtt-password, use in broker credentials as ${MQTT_BROKER_PASSWORD}>
  ENERGYID_PROVISIONING_KEY=<EnergyID provisioning key>
  ENERGYID_PROVISIONING_SECRET=<EnergyID provisioning secret>

Optional environment variables:
  EID_DEVICE_ALLOWLIST=                  (CSV of deviceIds; empty = allow all)
  EID_DEVICE_MAPPINGS_JSON=              (JSON mapping override)
  EID_BATCH_INTERVAL_S=60                (flush timer; each device still respects its upload policy, minimum 60s)
  EID_HELLO_TICK_S=5                     (how often to process pending /hello work)
  EID_HELLO_COOLDOWN_S=30
  EID_HELLO_MAX_RETRIES=20
  EID_CREDENTIAL_REFRESH_S=86400
  EID_MAX_BUFFER_POINTS=5000
  EID_MAX_BATCH_SIZE=1000

Device metric mapping is config-driven. Set EID_DEVICE_MAPPINGS_JSON to override defaults; see the comment node "Metric mapping quick guide" on the tab.

Import note: the broker host and port use ${MQTT_BROKER_HOST} and ${MQTT_BROKER_PORT}. MQTT credentials are intentionally not exported; set the broker username/password after import, or use ${MQTT_BROKER_USER} and ${MQTT_BROKER_PASSWORD} in the broker credentials fields. Prefer global/process environment variables for real secrets, not flow-level values in JSON that may be shared later. Configure TLS/certificate options in the MQTT broker node if your broker requires custom settings.

name: STEP 4: Ops (registry report, unclaimed list, reset)
Route + extract + register + buffer
Pop next unclaimed device
Store creds / capture claim URL
Fan-out one msg per claimed device with buffered readings
Check + handle 401
Build registry summary
Build unclaimed CSV
Wipe globals (DESTRUCTIVE)
Metric mapping quick guide
info:
Metric mapping quick guide

Edit EID_DEVICE_MAPPINGS_JSON on the tab to choose what gets sent. Leave empty to use defaults.

Example:
[{"topic":"tele/+/SENSOR","deviceId":"tasmota-${1}","deviceName":"Tasmota ${1}","deviceType":"tasmota","channels":[{"key":"el","path":"ENERGY.Total","scale":1},{"key":"pwr","path":"ENERGY.Power","scale":0.001}]}]

Standard EnergyID keys: el, el-i, pwr, pwr-i, gas, pv, wind, chp, dh, dc, solar, ev, bat, bat-i, heat, dw.

Use Show unmapped topics to find new MQTT topics or payloads that do not match a mapping.
